page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m2.6xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the ecs.u2i family, powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, this instance provides 24 vCPUs without hyperthreading, ensuring predictable compute performance for parallel processing tasks.

The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This allocation positions the server as a compute-optimized resource, prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u2i-c1m2.6xlarge offers a high density of dedicated Intel Xeon cores relative to its memory footprint. This makes it a resource-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not demand large RAM allocations or local SSD storage, avoiding the premium associated with over-provisioned memory.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include batch processing, web serving, distributed compute nodes, and CPU-intensive microservices. It is also suitable for CI/CD build runners and other parallelized execution tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores.
